question: Design and implement back end of a video streaming web site. It should provide all operations for users to watch videos online. It should also be able to deal with videos of small and large size, support streaming in different resolutions, give users the ability to pause, restart, skip forwards and backwards. Take into account risks of variable connection quality, potentially very large upload and download sizes, and concurrency.

paraphraser.0.0: Design and implement back end of a video streaming web site. It should provide all operations for users to watch videos online. It should also be able to deal with videos of small and large size, support streaming in different resolutions, give users the ability to pause, restart, skip forwards and backwards. Take into account risks of variable connection quality, potentially very large upload and download sizes, and concurrency.
paraphraser.0.25: Design and deploy the back end for a video streaming website. It should provide all operations for users to watch videos online. It also needs to be able to deal with videos of small and large sizes, support streaming in different resolutions, give users the ability to pause, restart, skip forwards and backwards. Consider risks of variable connection quality, potentially very large upload and download sizes, and concurrency.
paraphraser.0.6: Develop and execute the server-side for a video streaming website. It needs to offer all functions for users to view videos online. It must also handle both small and large videos, enable streaming in multiple resolutions, and allow users to pause, replay, fast forward, and rewind. Consider challenges of fluctuating connection quality, potentially very big upload and download sizes, and parallel processing.
paraphraser.0.9: The task is to design and build the server-side infrastructure for a video streaming web application. The system has to enable all user operations for watching videos online, manage video files regardless of size (small or large), enable different resolutions for streaming, and give controls like pause, replay, forward, and rewind. Important considerations also include inconsistent network quality, potentially massive file sizes, and concurrent usage.
